args.claim: Rust’s ownership and borrowing model, combined with its `Send`/`Sync` traits and lack of a data-race-prone shared mutable state in safe code, makes data races impossible in safe Rust, whereas Go’s type system allows data races in otherwise safe concurrent code and relies on a separate race detector tool to find them.
args.quote: Go’s memory safety can be violated in purely safe concurrent code (e.g., racy slice sharing between goroutines), whereas Rust’s type system and `Send`/`Sync` traits make such data races impossible without `unsafe`.
